expectations.text: Flags every any usage in the file
expectations.evidence: Finding #2 identifies all four: data (line 12), result (line 13), getConfig() return (line 35), user in formatUser (line 39).
Finding #3 calls out data as UserData on line 16 as an unsafe cast without a type guard.
Finding #1 explicitly flags the default export on line 12.
Finding #6 flags shouldValidate: boolean, includeMetadata: boolean and recommends a named options object.
Finding #4 calls out config.settings!.timeout on line 23 with correct reasoning about the crash path.

expectations.text: Flags the parallel type definition drift (role: string vs literal union)
expectations.evidence: Finding #5 flags the unsafe cast on line 40 but does not identify the structural drift between UserData.role: string in the interface and the literal union used in the cast.

expectations.text: Uses the confidence framework: findings categorized as high/moderate/low confidence
expectations.evidence: The review is a flat numbered list with no high/moderate/low confidence categorization anywhere.

expectations.text: Maps findings to specific standard sections from the skill
expectations.evidence: No standard section names or references appear in the review. Findings float without traceability.

expectations.text: Does NOT flag formatting or import order issues
expectations.evidence: All eight findings address substantive type safety or structural issues only.

assertion_critique: Assertion 6: The review catches the symptom (unsafe cast) but misses the root structural issue -- the interface definition itself is wrong.
Assertion 7: The confidence framework is a core output structure of the skill. The review ignores it entirely.
Assertion 8: Without section mapping, the output is not auditable against the standard.
